heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: In human history, malaria has killed more people than:
correct_option: Any other disease

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: In the late 19th century, which microorganism was found in the blood of malarial patients?
correct_option: Plasmodium

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: Who first discovered the microorganism Plasmodium in the blood of a malarial patient?
correct_option: Laveran

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: The hypothesis formed for Biological Problem 1 (cause of malaria) was:
option1: Plasmodium is the cause of malaria
correct_option: Plasmodium is the cause of malaria

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: The deduction made from the hypothesis 'Plasmodium is the cause of malaria' was:
option1: If Plasmodium causes malaria, then all malarial patients should have Plasmodium in their blood
option2: If marshy water causes malaria, then patients near marshes will have Plasmodium
option3: If quinine kills Plasmodium, then all patients will recover
option4: If mosquitoes transmit Plasmodium, then mosquitoes should carry it
correct_option: If Plasmodium causes malaria, then all malarial patients should have Plasmodium in their blood

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: To test the deduction in Problem 1, biologists examined blood samples of:
correct_option: 100 malarial patients and 100 healthy persons

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: In the Problem 1 experiment, which was the control group?
correct_option: Healthy persons

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
name: The result of the Problem 1 experiment was:
option1: No Plasmodium was found in any patient
option2: Plasmodium was found in all healthy persons
option3: Most malarial patients had Plasmodium in their blood, proving the hypothesis
option4: Quinine was found in the blood of patients
correct_option: Most malarial patients had Plasmodium in their blood, proving the hypothesis

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
subheading: Biological Problem 2: How Plasmodium Enters Human Blood
name: In 1883, physician A.F.A. King listed how many observations about malaria?

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
subheading: Biological Problem 2: How Plasmodium Enters Human Blood
name: Which observation was made by A.F.A. King regarding malaria?
option3: People sleeping under fine mosquito nets had less chance of getting malaria
option4: Quinine kills the parasite in the body
correct_option: People sleeping under fine mosquito nets had less chance of getting malaria

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
subheading: Biological Problem 2: How Plasmodium Enters Human Blood
name: People who slept near a smoky fire usually did not get malaria because:
option1: Smoke kills Plasmodium in their blood
option2: Fire increases temperature and Plasmodium are killed in air
option3: Mosquitoes cannot tolerate smoke and are repelled
option4: Smoke kills Plasmodium present in mosquitoes
correct_option: Mosquitoes cannot tolerate smoke and are repelled

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
subheading: Biological Problem 2: How Plasmodium Enters Human Blood
name: The hypothesis formed by A.F.A. King for how Plasmodium enters human blood was:
option2: Mosquitoes transmit Plasmodium and are involved in the spread of malaria
option4: Air in marshy areas spreads Plasmodium
correct_option: Mosquitoes transmit Plasmodium and are involved in the spread of malaria

heading: Malaria – An Example of Biological Method
subheading: Biological Problem 2: How Plasmodium Enters Human Blood
name: The deduction from King's hypothesis was:
option1: If mosquitoes are involved in spreading malaria, then Plasmodium should be present in mosquitoes
option2: If Plasmodium is in marshy water, then drinking it causes malaria
option3: If quinine is effective, then Plasmodium must be killed by it
option4: If smoke repels mosquitoes, then people near fire don't get malaria
correct_option: If mosquitoes are involved in spreading malaria, then Plasmodium should be present in mosquitoes
